mappings.rationale: Covers economic growth theory, productivity, and the relationship between growth and development.
Directly covers economic growth in developing countries, theories of development, and economic planning (subsections A.4 and B.4).
Covers historical patterns of economic growth and the causes of divergent development paths.
Covers technology and markets as factors influencing economic growth (subsection A.1).
Covers the dynamics of capitalist growth including capital supply, labour markets, and productivity.
Covers economic planning in Communist countries and centrally planned growth models (subsection B.2).
Covers socialist approaches to economic planning and growth.
Covers demand-determined models of growth and government fiscal policies for stability (subsection A.2.a).
Covers supply-side economic growth theories and market-oriented approaches to planning.
Covers the social costs and benefits of economic growth and sustainable development (subsection A.3).
Covers the environmental costs of economic growth and policies for sustainable development (subsection A.3).
Covers how globalization affects economic growth patterns and development planning across countries.
Covers economic growth in major developing countries and their development strategies (subsection A.4).
Covers a pivotal period of economic growth driven by technology, capital, and labour force changes.
Provides a case study of economic planning and growth strategies in a formerly Communist country.
Covers Malthusian theories about the relationship between population growth and economic development.
Covers foundational theories of economic growth including the wealth of nations and productivity.
Covers Marxist theories of economic development, capital accumulation, and the dynamics of growth.
Covers the environmental consequences of economic growth, relevant to social costs and benefits (subsection A.3).
Covers mathematical growth models used in economic planning and forecasting (subsection A.2.b).
Examines the economic dimensions of environmental inequality including capitalism, poverty, and corporate accountability, relevant to this section's coverage of economic growth and its consequences.
